Entrepreneurship is the process by which individuals pursue opportunities without regard to resources they currently control.

Key definitions:

  • The activity of setting up a business or businesses, taking on financial risk in the hope of making a profit
  • Involves creating a business or businesses while building it to generate a profit
  • Entrepreneurship is what people do to take their career and dreams into their hands and lead it in the direction of their own choice

Who is an Entrepreneur?

An Entrepreneur is the person who organizes the other factors of production (land, labour, and capital) to set up a business to make a profit.

Qualities of an Entrepreneur

  • Creative and innovative
  • Opportunity seeking
  • Persistence
  • Creative thinking
  • Risk-taking ability / Moderate risk taker
  • Self-discipline
  • Resolute motivation and passionate
  • Responsible and self-disciplined
  • Initiative taking
  • Achievement oriented
  • High level of Energy
  • Networker

Importance of Entrepreneurship in TVET

  • Can improve the standard of living and create wealth
  • TVET graduates become work-ready – they gain professional knowledge and get the opportunity to apply the knowledge
  • Helps TVET graduates as employees or as entrepreneurs setting up their own businesses
  • Helps to reduce unemployment
  • Aids in the developmental agenda of the country

sessions.title: Employability Skills in TVET
indicators: Explain Employability Skills
sessions.content:
Meaning of Employability Skills

Employability skills are the transferable skills needed by individuals to make them 'employable'.

Key definitions:

  • Along with good technical understanding and subject knowledge, employers often look for a set of skills that they want from an employee
  • Refers to your ability to gain initial employment or being capable of getting and keeping the work
  • Jobs or soft skills or transferable skills and capabilities needed by a graduate besides their technical know-how
  • The essential skills, personal qualities, and values that enable you to thrive in any workplace

Employability Skills Useful in TVET

1. Personal initiative and being self-motivated

  • Being creative and having new ideas of your own
  • Showing a strong personal drive and not waiting to be told to do things

2. Ability to learn and adapt

  • To be able to acquire new skills and knowledge in new situations
  • Learn from your mistakes and others
  • Enable you to change ways to suit what is right for the benefit of the individual and employer

3. Problem-Solving Skills

  • The ability to perceive and understand a problem by breaking it down into smaller constituents
  • Identifying key issues, implications, and identifying solutions
  • The application of skills, ideas, and know-how to solve problems on a task within a reasonable time

4. Working Under Pressure and Meeting Deadlines

  • The ability to work and meet deadlines under stressful conditions
  • The ability to recognize, understand and manage one's own emotions and others' to positively achieve desired outcomes

5. Organizational Skills

  • The ability to plan and be methodical in monitoring and meeting targets and deadlines

6. Team Working

  • Working together with other people from different backgrounds, disciplines, and expertise to meet goals

7. Appreciating Unity in Diversity

  • Knowing the value of diversity and what it can bring
  • Understanding and being considerate of the different needs of different individuals

8. Negotiation Skills

  • The ability to know what you want to achieve and take into account other people's feelings
  • Getting to a good conclusion on what you both need

9. Confidence

  • Believing in yourself, your colleagues, and the institution or company you work for without being arrogant

10. Communication Skills

  • The ability to explain what you mean clearly and concisely (oral, written, presenting, listening)
  • Enables you to pass on the right information without distortion
